steps.title: Identify complementary angles in the right triangle
steps.content: Triangle ACE has a right angle at E. Since the sum of angles in a triangle is 180°, we have: $$A + C + 90° = 180°$$ Therefore, $A + C = 90°$, meaning angles A and C are complementary angles.

steps.title: Apply the complementary angle identity
steps.content: For complementary angles, the sine of one angle equals the cosine of the other: $$\sin C = \cos A$$ Since we're given that $\cos A = 0.55$, we have: $$\sin C = 0.55$$ This matches choice C — C is correct.
